auth_notes: Requires creating a developer account and an application in the Procore Developer Portal to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret. Supports Authorization Code flow for user-level access and Client Credentials for service-level access.
rate_limits: Standard limit is 3,600 requests per hour per OAuth token.
gotchas: Pagination uses Link headers (RFC 5988), which requires manual parsing in n8n to handle multi-page data sets.
The API is strictly versioned; some resources are on v1.0 while others are on v1.1, requiring careful endpoint verification.
Sandbox and Production environments require separate Client IDs and Secrets.
research_notes: Implementation requires the n8n HTTP Request node. Authentication should be configured using the 'OAuth2' credential type in n8n, pointing to Procore's identity server (login.procore.com). Webhooks must be configured via the Procore Webhooks API or the Developer Portal UI to send events to an n8n Webhook node.
